name: last30days
tagline: Multi-source social/web research engine (Reddit, X, YouTube, TikTok, HN, Polymarket, GitHub, web) that synthesizes a 'last 30 days'.
verdict: Unusually thorough on disclosure and safety: a dedicated Security & Permissions section lists every network destination and credential path, browser-cookie reads are consent-gated and never persisted to disk, secrets are masked/never echoed, and config writes are append-only. The tool sends legitimate research queries to APIs the user opted into — no.

highlights.summary: yt-dlp and the author's own 'digg-pp-cli' (via npx) are installed with no version pinning (Manual Setup Guide, ~line 583/587).
No 'NOT for' / out-of-scope text anywhere despite covering a very broad range of research query types that could misfire on adjacent asks.

2148-line SKILL.md packs dozens of inline 'named failure mode'/'observed violation' case studies and full worked examples that are only sometimes needed, with obvious split points to reference files n
